item.title: Towards a Medical AI Scientist
item.description: Autonomous systems that generate scientific hypotheses, conduct experiments, and draft manuscripts have recently emerged as a promising paradigm for accelerating discovery. However, existing AI Scientists remain largely domain-agnostic,...

item.title: ChartNet: A Million-Scale, High-Quality Multimodal Dataset for Robust Chart Understanding
item.description: Understanding charts requires models to jointly reason over geometric visual patterns, structured numerical data, and natural language -- a capability where current vision-language models (VLMs) remain limited. We introduce ChartNet, a h...

item.title: On-the-fly Repulsion in the Contextual Space for Rich Diversity in Diffusion Transformers
item.description: Modern Text-to-Image (T2I) diffusion models have achieved remarkable semantic alignment, yet they often suffer from a significant lack of variety, converging on a narrow set of visual solutions for any given prompt. This typicality bias...

item.title: Kernel-Smith: A Unified Recipe for Evolutionary Kernel Optimization
item.description: We present Kernel-Smith, a framework for high-performance GPU kernel and operator generation that combines a stable evaluation-driven evolutionary agent with an evolution-oriented post-training recipe. On the agent side, Kernel-Smith mai...

item.title: A Comparative Study in Surgical AI: Datasets, Foundation Models, and Barriers to Med-AGI
item.description: Recent Artificial Intelligence (AI) models have matched or exceeded human experts in several benchmarks of biomedical task performance, but have lagged behind on surgical image-analysis benchmarks. Since surgery requires integrating disp...

item.title: Emergent Social Intelligence Risks in Generative Multi-Agent Systems
item.description: Multi-agent systems composed of large generative models are rapidly moving from laboratory prototypes to real-world deployments, where they jointly plan, negotiate, and allocate shared resources to solve complex tasks. While such systems...

item.title: GEditBench v2: A Human-Aligned Benchmark for General Image Editing
item.description: Recent advances in image editing have enabled models to handle complex instructions with impressive realism. However, existing evaluation frameworks lag behind: current benchmarks suffer from narrow task coverage, while standard metrics...

item.title: ResAdapt: Adaptive Resolution for Efficient Multimodal Reasoning
item.description: Multimodal Large Language Models (MLLMs) achieve stronger visual understanding by scaling input fidelity, yet the resulting visual token growth makes jointly sustaining high spatial resolution and long temporal context prohibitive. We ar...

item.title: DreamLite: A Lightweight On-Device Unified Model for Image Generation and Editing
item.description: Diffusion models have made significant progress in both text-to-image (T2I) generation and text-guided image editing. However, these models are typically built with billions of parameters, leading to high latency and increased deployment...

item.title: STRIDE: When to Speak Meets Sequence Denoising for Streaming Video Understanding
item.description: Recent progress in video large language models (Video-LLMs) has enabled strong offline reasoning over long and complex videos. However, real-world deployments increasingly require streaming perception and proactive interaction, where vid...

item.title: Gen-Searcher: Reinforcing Agentic Search for Image Generation
item.description: Recent image generation models have shown strong capabilities in generating high-fidelity and photorealistic images. However, they are fundamentally constrained by frozen internal knowledge, thus often failing on real-world scenarios tha...

item.title: EpochX: Building the Infrastructure for an Emergent Agent Civilization
item.description: General-purpose technologies reshape economies less by improving individual tools than by enabling new ways to organize production and coordination. We believe AI agents are approaching a similar inflection point: as foundation models ma...

item.title: PRBench: End-to-end Paper Reproduction in Physics Research
item.description: AI agents powered by large language models exhibit strong reasoning and problem-solving capabilities, enabling them to assist scientific research tasks such as formula derivation and code generation. However, whether these agents can rel...

item.title: Make Geometry Matter for Spatial Reasoning
item.description: Empowered by large-scale training, vision-language models (VLMs) achieve strong image and video understanding, yet their ability to perform spatial reasoning in both static scenes and dynamic videos remains limited. Recent advances try t...

item.title: ImagenWorld: Stress-Testing Image Generation Models with Explainable Human Evaluation on Open-ended Real-World Tasks
item.description: Advances in diffusion, autoregressive, and hybrid models have enabled high-quality image synthesis for tasks such as text-to-image, editing, and reference-guided composition. Yet, existing benchmarks remain limited, either focus on isola...

item.title: HandX: Scaling Bimanual Motion and Interaction Generation
item.description: Synthesizing human motion has advanced rapidly, yet realistic hand motion and bimanual interaction remain underexplored. Whole-body models often miss the fine-grained cues that drive dexterous behavior, finger articulation, contact timin...

item.title: SEAR: Schema-Based Evaluation and Routing for LLM Gateways
item.description: Evaluating production LLM responses and routing requests across providers in LLM gateways requires fine-grained quality signals and operationally grounded decisions. To address this gap, we present SEAR, a schema-based evaluation and rou...

item.title: AdaptToken: Entropy-based Adaptive Token Selection for MLLM Long Video Understanding
item.description: Long video understanding remains challenging for Multi-modal Large Language Models (MLLMs) due to high memory costs and context-length limits. Prior approaches mitigate this by scoring and selecting frames/tokens within short clips, but...

item.title: KAT-Coder-V2 Technical Report
item.description: We present KAT-Coder-V2, an agentic coding model developed by the KwaiKAT team at Kuaishou. KAT-Coder-V2 adopts a "Specialize-then-Unify" paradigm that decomposes agentic coding into five expert domains - SWE, WebCoding, Terminal, WebSea...

item.title: MOOZY: A Patient-First Foundation Model for Computational Pathology
item.description: Computational pathology needs whole-slide image (WSI) foundation models that transfer across diverse clinical tasks, yet current approaches remain largely slide-centric, often depend on private data and expensive paired-report supervisio...

item.title: TAPS: Task Aware Proposal Distributions for Speculative Sampling
item.description: Speculative decoding accelerates autoregressive generation by letting a lightweight draft model propose future tokens that a larger target model then verifies in parallel. In practice, however, draft models are usually trained on broad g...

item.title: On Token's Dilemma: Dynamic MoE with Drift-Aware Token Assignment for Continual Learning of Large Vision Language Models
item.description: Multimodal Continual Instruction Tuning aims to continually enhance Large Vision Language Models (LVLMs) by learning from new data without forgetting previously acquired knowledge. Mixture of Experts (MoE) architectures naturally facilit...

item.title: MuSEAgent: A Multimodal Reasoning Agent with Stateful Experiences
item.description: Research agents have recently achieved significant progress in information seeking and synthesis across heterogeneous textual and visual sources. In this paper, we introduce MuSEAgent, a multimodal reasoning agent that enhances decision-...

item.title: Marco DeepResearch: Unlocking Efficient Deep Research Agents via Verification-Centric Design
item.description: Deep research agents autonomously conduct open-ended investigations, integrating complex information retrieval with multi-step reasoning across diverse sources to solve real-world problems. To sustain this capability on long-horizon task...

item.title: Story2Proposal: A Scaffold for Structured Scientific Paper Writing
item.description: Generating scientific manuscripts requires maintaining alignment between narrative reasoning, experimental evidence, and visual artifacts across the document lifecycle. Existing language-model generation pipelines rely on unconstrained t...

item.title: MolmoPoint: Better Pointing for VLMs with Grounding Tokens
item.description: Grounding has become a fundamental capability of vision-language models (VLMs). Most existing VLMs point by generating coordinates as part of their text output, which requires learning a complicated coordinate system and results in a hig...

item.title: HISA: Efficient Hierarchical Indexing for Fine-Grained Sparse Attention
item.description: Token-level sparse attention mechanisms, exemplified by DeepSeek Sparse Attention (DSA), achieve fine-grained key selection by scoring every historical token for each query using a lightweight indexer, and then computing attention only o...

item.title: Unified Number-Free Text-to-Motion Generation Via Flow Matching
item.description: Generative models excel at motion synthesis for a fixed number of agents but struggle to generalize with variable agents. Based on limited, domain-specific data, existing methods employ autoregressive models to generate motion recursivel...

item.title: Density-aware Soft Context Compression with Semi-Dynamic Compression Ratio
item.description: Soft context compression reduces the computational workload of processing long contexts in LLMs by encoding long context into a smaller number of latent tokens. However, existing frameworks apply uniform compression ratios, failing to ac...

item.title: Superintelligence and Law
item.description: The prospect of artificial superintelligence -- AI agents that can generally outperform humans in cognitive tasks and economically valuable activities -- will transform the legal order as we know it. Operating autonomously or under only...

item.title: Think over Trajectories: Leveraging Video Generation to Reconstruct GPS Trajectories from Cellular Signaling
item.description: Mobile devices continuously interact with cellular base stations, generating massive volumes of signaling records that provide broad coverage for understanding human mobility. However, such records offer only coarse location cues (e.g.,...

item.title: A Neural Score-Based Particle Method for the Vlasov-Maxwell-Landau System
item.description: Plasma modeling is central to the design of nuclear fusion reactors, yet simulating collisional plasma kinetics from first principles remains a formidable computational challenge: the Vlasov-Maxwell-Landau (VML) system describes six-dime...

item.title: INSID3: Training-Free In-Context Segmentation with DINOv3
item.description: In-context segmentation (ICS) aims to segment arbitrary concepts, e.g., objects, parts, or personalized instances, given one annotated visual examples. Existing work relies on (i) fine-tuning vision foundation models (VFMs), which improv...

item.title: Video Generation Models as World Models: Efficient Paradigms, Architectures and Algorithms
item.description: The rapid evolution of video generation has enabled models to simulate complex physical dynamics and long-horizon causalities, positioning them as potential world simulators. However, a critical gap still remains between the theoretical...

item.title: Text Data Integration
item.description: Data comes in many forms. From a shallow perspective, they can be viewed as being either in structured (e.g., as a relation, as key-value pairs) or unstructured (e.g., text, image) formats. So far, machines have been fairly good at proce...
